>
> —[Axell Florent](wiki:Axell_Florent) to [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon)

> [Claw Isle](wiki:Claw_Isle) was but lightly garrisoned, its castle reputedly stuffed with [Myrish](wiki:Myr) carpets, [Volantene](wiki:Volantis) glass, [gold](wiki:Gold) and [silver](wiki:Silver) plate, jeweled cups, magnificent [hawks](wiki:Hawk), an axe of [Valyrian steel](wiki:Valyrian_steel), [a horn](wiki:House_Celtigar_horn) that could summon monsters from the deep, chests of [rubies](wiki:Ruby), and more [wines](wiki:Wine) than a man could drink in a hundred years. Though Celtigar had shown the world a niggardly face, he had never stinted on his own comforts.(wiki:Ardrian_Celtigar.cite_ref-Rasos36.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-5)
>
> —thoughts of [Davos Seaworth](wiki:Davos_Seaworth)

> I don't doubt Lord Celtigar bent the knee to the boy [Joffrey](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon). He is an old done man, who wants no more than to end his days in his castle, drinking his fine [wine](wiki:Wine) out of his jeweled cups. Yet he came when you called, sire. Came, with his ships and swords. He stood by you at [Storm's End](wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End_(299)) when Lord [Renly](wiki:Renly_Baratheon) came down on us, and his ships sailed up the [Blackwater](wiki:Blackwater_Rush). His men fought for you, killed for you, [burned for you](wiki:Battle_of_the_Blackwater).(wiki:Ardrian_Celtigar.cite_ref-Rasos36.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-6)
>
> —[Davos Seaworth](wiki:Davos_Seaworth) to [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon)

> When I die impoverished, my wives and concubines will curse you, Onion Lord. Lord Celtigar had many fine [wines](wiki:Wine) that now I am not tasting, a sea [eagle](wiki:Eagle) he had trained to fly from the wrist, and [a magic horn](wiki:House_Celtigar_horn) to summon [krakens](wiki:Kraken) from the deep. Very useful such a horn would be, to pull down [Tyroshi](wiki:Tyrosh) and other vexing creatures.(wiki:Ardrian_Celtigar.cite_ref-Rasos54.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-2)

This is a long list of 55 candidates. Let me go through them systematically, looking at the evidence in the prose:

Lines 1-9 are about possessions and materials (crabs, garnets, horn, kraken, red-claw, valyrian-steel). These are from "Appearances & Description" section.

Let me map each:

1. **crab** - "Lord Celtigar owns a mantle patterned with red [crabs]" → OWNS ardrian-celtigar → crab (but crab is likely concept/object.creature, not a possession edge)
2. **currency** - "known for his avarice and his [wealth]" → This is just mentioned as a reputation; the edge would be reputed-as or possesses-wealth, not an edge to currency itself
3. **davos-seaworth** - "He looks down upon the lowborn Ser [Davos Seaworth]" → DISTRUSTS or RESENTS or PERCEIVED_AS
4. **garnet** - "patterned with red crabs picked out in [garnets]" → possession of garnets in the mantle
5. **house-celtigar-horn** - "and [a horn] capable of summoning [krakens]" → OWNS the horn
6. **kraken** - "capable of summoning [krakens]" → This is about the horn's capability, not about owning krakens
7. **red-claw** - "His ship is the ornate *[Red Claw]*" → OWNS or CAPTAIN_OF or OWNS the Red Claw
8. **stannis-baratheon** (line 8) - "though Lord [Stannis Baratheon] is dismissive of Ardrian's own opinions" → DISTRUSTS or RESENTS
9. **valyrian-steel** - "include a [Valyrian steel] axe" → OWNS the axe; MADE_OF for the material relationship


10. **battle-of-the-blackwater** - "The Red Crab is captured at the [Battle of the Blackwater]" → FIGHTS_IN or PARTICIPATES_IN or CAPTURED_IN (but no CAPTURED_IN edge type exists in vocab)
11. **dragonstone** - "Ardrian attends Stannis's feast at [Dragonstone]" → ATTENDS dragonstone (wait, ATTENDS targets events not locations) or LOCATED_AT
12. **faith-of-the-seven** - "burns the statues of the [Seven]" → This suggests a religious action, possibly OPPOSES or REJECTS the faith

, but Ardrian isn't the one burning them—Melisandre is. This is just a mention.
13. **iron-throne** - "when he lays claim to the [Iron Throne]" → No direct relationship for Ardrian here either.
14. **joffrey-baratheon** - "publicly bends the knee to King [Joffrey I Baratheon]" → SWORN_TO or KNELT_BEFORE would work, though the reverse relationship might be COMMANDED.

Let me look back at the actual candidate sentences. Row 10 shows "The Red Crab is captured at the [LINK]" for battle-of-the-blackwater, Row 11 has "Ardrian attends Stannis's feast at [LINK]" for dragonstone, and Row 12 starts with "He has a coughing fit when [Melisandre](wiki:Melisandre) burns the statues of the [LINK] taken from the [sept at Dragonstone]" for faith-of-the-seven. Row 13 mentions "when he lays claim to the [LINK] during the [War of the Five Kings]" for iron-throne, Row 14 says "and publicly bends the knee to King [LINK]" for joffrey-baratheon, and Row 15 repeats the coughing fit with Melisandre as the link. Row 16 focuses on the statues being taken from sept-at-dragonstone, Row 17 shows "Lord Celtigar participates in Stannis's [LINK]" for siege-of-storms-end-299, Row 18 has the claim to the throne during war-of-the-five-kings, and Row 19 just links to war-of-the-five-kings itself.

Moving into the Storm of Swords section, I see Axell Florent appears in prose discussing Celtigar's fears of reprisals, Davos Seaworth objects to some plan, and there's a location reference to King's Landing where Ardrian stays. Margaery Tyrell appears at Joffrey's wedding, Ardrian attends the purple-wedding event, and Tyrion Lannister is mentioned when Ardrian testifies against him—though I need to determine the exact edge type for that last connection.

Now examining the quotations and narrative sections more carefully: the crabs mentioned are just decorative patterns on Celtigar's mantle, not actual creatures Ardrian owns, so that's not a real edge. References to his avarice and wealth describe character traits rather than a relationship to currency itself. However, Ardrian's perception that he looks down upon the lowborn Ser Davos Seaworth does establish a relationship—something like REPUTED_AS or PERCEIVED_AS captures how Ardrian views Davos.

The manual specifies PERCEIVED_AS requires a specific POV character observing another, but the wiki uses omniscient narration rather than a single perspective. DISTRUSTS or RESENTS would work better here; "looks down upon" suggests disdain, so I'll use DISTRUSTS. That gives me an edge: ardrian-celtigar DISTRUSTS davos-seaworth. Moving to the next candidate, garnets appear as decorative elements on the mantle, similar to the crabs—just ornamentation, not something Ardrian actually possesses or relates to meaningfully.

The horn capable of summoning krakens is different. Ardrian owns this artifact, so that's a clear OWNS relationship. But krakens themselves are only mentioned as what the horn can summon—there's no direct connection between Ardrian and krakens, so that's just a mention to skip.

For the Red Claw, Ardrian's ship, I need to decide between OWNS and CAPTAIN_OF. The prose says "His ship," which suggests ownership, but captains also command vessels. Looking at the vocabulary definitions, CAPTAIN_OF is specifically for commanding a named vessel, while OWNS covers possession more broadly. The manual notes that captains may or may not own what they command, so these are distinct. Since the text emphasizes possession with "His ship," OWNS seems like the right fit, though CAPTAIN_OF could work if the context leans more toward command.

For Stannis Baratheon, the passage describes him as dismissive of Ardrian's opinions. This is Stannis's attitude toward Ardrian, so I should consider how Ardrian might perceive this dismissal—either through RESENT, DISTRUSTS, or a relationship edge capturing Stannis's view.
